description_long: This is a vintage-style deep-sea diving helmet, characterized by a large, rounded dome made of heavily weathered and corroded metal with patches of green and brown oxidation. The front features a prominent circular window with a protective metal grid, flanked by two smaller round side ports, all rimmed in brass or gold-colored metal. The helmet's base is wide and flared, designed to rest securely on the shoulders, and is adorned with an engraved anchor motif. The surface is rough and pitted, with visible signs of rust and marine growth, suggesting long-term exposure to water and harsh conditions. Brass bolts and fittings are distributed around the base and sides, and a rectangular vent or hatch is present on the top. The overall appearance is utilitarian and robust, evoking early 20th-century underwater exploration equipment.
description: A weathered, vintage deep-sea diving helmet with a domed, rusted metal body, brass-rimmed windows, and a flared base featuring an anchor engraving.
description_view_0: Side view showing the large circular window with a brass rim and the corroded, mottled surface texture.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the rounded dome and the wide, flared base with visible corrosion and no windows.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a similar circular window and brass detailing, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the main gridded window, two side ports, and the engraved anchor motif on the base.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: One rectangular vent or hatch on the top; no other visible ventilation openings.
attribute_graphic_design: Engraved anchor motif on the front base; otherwise mottled, corroded surface with no additional graphics.
